title: Ren_2022
study_design: retrospective cohort study with nomogram development and validation

sample_size.source_quote: 1676 patients with sepsis and lung infection were included in our study

mortality_predictors.predictor_variable: Nomogram (temperature, oxygenation index, age, BUN, lactate, GCS score, liver disease, cancer, organ transplantation, TnT, NLR, CRRT, MV, vasopressor use)
mortality_predictors.statistical_method: multivariate logistic regression with stepwise selection; AUROC comparison via Delong's test
mortality_predictors.auc: Training set AUROC: 0.743; Validation set AUROC: 0.746
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: temperature, oxygenation index, age, BUN, lactate, GCS score, liver disease, cancer, organ transplantation, TnT, NLR, CRRT, MV, vasopressor use
mortality_predictors.source_quote: in training set our nomogram' AUROC is higher than SOFA (0.743 vs 0.647) and SAPSII (0.743 vs 0.707); and in validation set nomogram' AUROC is higher than SOFA (0.746 vs 0.596) and SAPSII (0.746 vs 0.664)

mortality_predictors.auc: Training set AUROC: 0.647; Validation set AUROC: 0.596
mortality_predictors.source_quote: in training set our nomogram' AUROC is higher than SOFA (0.743 vs 0.647) and SAPSII (0.743 vs 0.707); and in validation set nomogram' AUROC is higher than SOFA (0.746 vs 0.596) and SAPSII (0.746 vs 0.664)

mortality_predictors.auc: Training set AUROC: 0.707; Validation set AUROC: 0.664
mortality_predictors.source_quote: in training set our nomogram' AUROC is higher than SOFA (0.743 vs 0.647) and SAPSII (0.743 vs 0.707); and in validation set nomogram' AUROC is higher than SOFA (0.746 vs 0.596) and SAPSII (0.746 vs 0.664)

mortality_predictors.effect_size: Training set NRI: 47.32% (95% CI: 36.08–70.46%); Validation set NRI: 36.42% (95% CI: 16.11–59.11%)
mortality_predictors.source_quote: The NRIs of the nomogram were 47.32% (95% C.I.:36.08–70.46%) and 36.42% (95% C.I.:16.11–59.11%) in the training and validation sets, respectively.
